title: Consolidate variable axes (weight; optical size where justified) across shipped families
context: After v1.0 of the accessibility-first Latin family ships (fonts-ship-104), M3 broadens and hardens the variable-font coverage across the portfolio. Variable axes reduce per-weight drawing cost and give downstream users flexibility; this task ensures the axis strategy is consistent, QA-green, and documented across all shipped families.
objective: Consolidate and document the variable-axis implementation across all shipped families: confirm the weight axis is consistent and QA-green, add an optical-size axis where technically justified with rationale, and ensure all static instance exports are correct and reproducibly buildable.
acceptanceCriteria: Weight axis is present and QA-green (no FontBakery FAIL) across all shipped families.
An optical-size axis is added where justified; rationale documented; if not added, the decision is recorded.
All static weight instances export correctly as OTF and TTF without errors.
Variable font builds pass FontBakery Universal + GF profiles (no FAIL) and HarfBuzz shaping diffs.
Reproducible build verified: clean-environment rebuild yields byte-stable variable TTF artifacts.
No secrets, tokens, or API keys are introduced in build scripts or CI configuration.
output: A pull request consolidating the variable-axis implementation across shipped families, with updated designspace files, axis rationale documentation, and all CI gates passing.
